title: Taraknath Temple
tagline: Taraknath Temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: The Taraknath temple, dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva worshiped as Tarakanatha, is a major pilgrimage spot in the town of Tarakeswar, West Bengal, India.
overview.description: The Taraknath temple, dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva worshiped as Tarakanatha, is a major pilgrimage spot in the town of Tarakeswar, West Bengal, India. Built in 1729, the temple is an atchala structure of Bengal temple architecture with a 'natmandir' in front. Close by are the shrines of Kali and Lakshmi Narayan. Dudhpukur, a tank to the north of the Shiva temple is believed to fulfil the prayers of those taking a dip in it.

topPlaces.name: Tarakeswar
topPlaces.description: Tarakeswar is a famous sacred city and a municipality in Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal. Tarakeswar is called "Baba Dham" or "The city of Shiva".

topPlaces.name: Tarakeswar (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Tarakeshwar is a panchayat samiti (community development block) in Chandannagore subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Pursurah (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Pursurah (also spelled Pursura) is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Arambag subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Pursurah
topPlaces.description: Pursurah (also spelled Pursura) is a village in the Pursurah CD block in the Arambag subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Rabindra Mahavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Rabindra Mahavidyalaya, (popularly known as Champadanga College) established in 1971, is a general degree college in Champadanga, in the Hooghly district, India. It offers undergraduate courses in arts, commerce and sciences.

topPlaces.name: Kulbatpur
topPlaces.description: Kulbatpur is a village in Pursurah community development block of Arambagh subdivision in Hooghly District in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is 7 km from Tarakeswar .

topPlaces.name: Champadanga
topPlaces.description: Champadanga is a Town in Tarakeswar CD Block in Chandannagore subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Kaikala (village)
topPlaces.description: Kaikala is a village in Hooghly District in the Indian state of West Bengal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Taraknath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Taraknath Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Taraknath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (51 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Taraknath Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Taraknath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Taraknath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Taraknath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Taraknath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tarakeswar, Tarakeswar (community development block), Pursurah (community development block), Pursurah, Rabindra Mahavidyalaya.
